advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Completed foundational study of the 14 major allergens recognized under EU food labeling law and the top 9 under US FDA guidelines. Began study of IgE-mediated allergy vs. non-IgE intolerance mechanisms.
research_findings: Catalogued 14 major allergen groups with cross-reactivity profiles and hidden source identification guides. Identified that peanut, tree nut, shellfish, and fin fish allergies are the most common causes of anaphylaxis.
current_focus_areas: Advancing through elimination diet and oral food challenge protocols; building allergen-safe meal planning frameworks; developing hidden allergen identification tools; coordinating with Food Allergy and Intolerance Assessment bot.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Appointed Department Leader of the Allergy & Intolerance Science department. Knowledge domains assigned from mind map covering histamine intolerance, lactose intolerance types, FODMAP phases, label reading, hidden allergen sources, cross-reactivity patterns, epinephrine dosing, anaphylaxis symptoms, allergy testing, emergency protocols, food intolerance types, common food allergens (9 major allergens), and 12 standalone clinical topics. Reporting daily progress to Copilot (who reports to starrm1 on request). Progress: 0%.
Progress update: Completed major allergen cataloguing and IgE vs. non-IgE mechanism study. Initiated elimination diet and oral food challenge protocol development. Progress advanced to 10%.
